title: Designer Quantum States in Magnetic Topological Insulator Multilayers
authors: Deyi Zhuo, Han Tay, Cui-Zu Chang
abstract: Magnetic topological insulators (TIs) provide a highly tunable platform for engineering quantum states that emerge from the interplay between topology and magnetism. In this review article, we summarize experimental progress over the past decade in designing magnetic TI multilayers by molecular beam epitaxy (MBE). By treating magnetically doped and undoped TI layers as topological Legos, we discuss how layer thickness, magnetic doping, heterostructure architecture, and stacking sequence can be used to control magnetic exchange gaps, interlayer coupling, and the Chern number C with atomic-layer precision. We first briefly review the realization of the C = 1 quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) effect in uniformly Cr-doped (Bi,Sb)2Te3 films in 2013 and uniformly V-doped (Bi,Sb)2Te3 films in 2015. We then discuss how Cr-doped and undoped (Bi,Sb)2Te3 layers can be combined to realize the C = 1 QAH effect in magnetically modulation-doped trilayers, including its extension into the three-dimensional (3D) regime. Next, we review the development of high-C QAH states, engineered plateau phase transitions, mesoscopic QAH devices, and electrical switching of chiral edge-current chirality. Finally, we discuss the realizations of axion insulator and C = 1/2 parity anomaly states in asymmetric magnetic TI trilayers. These advances establish magnetic TI multilayers as a versatile materials platform for creating new designer quantum states, including synthetic Weyl semimetal and QAH metal phases, and for probing the topological magnetoelectric effect in thick axion insulators and 3D QAH insulators.
summary: This review article details how magnetic topological insulator multilayers can be used as building blocks to design custom quantum states. By controlling layer thickness and magnetic doping, researchers can engineer complex phases like high-Chern-number insulators and axion insulators. This platform provides a versatile toolkit for studying topological magnetoelectric effects and 3D topological physics.
main_problem: The challenge of engineering and controlling specific topological quantum states, such as high Chern number QAH states, axion insulators, and parity anomaly states, by manipulating the interplay between magnetism and topology.
main_result: The review demonstrates that magnetic TI multilayers act as 'topological Legos,' allowing for the precise engineering of designer quantum states, including high Chern number (C up to 5) states, axion insulators, and synthetic Weyl semimetals.
method: The paper reviews experimental progress using Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE) for atomic-layer precision growth and transport measurements (Hall resistance and longitudinal resistance) to identify quantized states.
model_or_system: Magnetic Topological Insulator (TI) multilayers and heterostructures, specifically using Cr-doped or V-doped (Bi,Sb)2Te3 layers combined with undoped (Bi,Sb)2Te3 layers as building blocks.
key_observables: Chern number (C), Hall resistance (rho_yx), longitudinal resistance (rho_xx), Hall conductance (sigma_xy), and magnetic exchange gaps.
important_parameters: Layer thickness (in Quintuple Layers), magnetic doping concentration, magnetization alignment (parallel vs. antiparallel), and interlayer coupling.
main_assumptions: The review focuses on the regime of low temperatures (down to 25 mK) and assumes high-quality growth via MBE to minimize disorder.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the C=1 QAH effect in doped films; Figure 2 illustrates the 'Topological Legos' concept; Figure 3 details QAH in trilayers; Figure 4 shows high Chern number (C=1 to 5) engineering; Figure 5/6 covers phase transitions and SOT switching.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the realization of C=1 QAH effect, progresses to modulation-doped trilayers and high-C states, moves to mesoscopic devices and electrical switching, and concludes with the realization of axion insulators, parity anomaly states, and 3D topological phases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
